title: Cyathea alatissima
intro: Cyathea alatissima is a species of tree fern, a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Cyatheaceae and is native to New Caledonia. The health benefits of Cyathea alatissima include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Cyathea alatissima is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Cyathea alatissima is classified as a species of tree fern within the family Cyatheaceae. This classification places it within the division Pteridophyta, which encompasses all ferns. The genus Cyathea is further categorized within the order Cyatheales. Within the kingdom Plantae, Cyathea alatissima is a member of the clade Monilophyta. The exact classification of Cyathea alatissima may be subject to revision as new research emerges.
uses: Cyathea alatissima, a species of tree fern, is used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. However, more research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and potential side effects. Further studies are required to fully understand the potential benefits and limitations of using Cyathea alatissima in medicine.

parts: Cyathea alatissima is a species of tree fern native to New Zealand, and like many other tree ferns, it is believed to have some medicinal properties.
